name: loop-engineering-go
description: Loop Engineering for Go projects — rules, workflows, and verification tools (build, vet, test, lint) for use with naitv-mcp + Continue.
author: madicen

entries.name: call-initialize-on-start
entries.body:
At the start of every session, call the initialize tool from naitv-mcp before doing any work.
This loads the user's standing instructions, preferences, and context. Treat the result as
binding for the entire session. Do not skip this step even for small tasks.

entries.name: verify-before-done
entries.body:
After writing or editing Go code, always run the verification sequence before declaring
work complete:
  1. build  — confirms the package compiles
  2. vet    — flags common correctness issues
  3. test   — runs the full test suite

Only mark work as done when all three pass. If any fail, fix the reported issues and
re-run from step 1. Never leave failing checks for the user to discover.

entries.name: propose-learnings
entries.body:
When you discover something during a session that would help future sessions — a pattern,
a preference, a gotcha, a workflow — propose it via add_entry so the user can approve it.

Good candidates:
  - A rule the user corrected you on
  - A project-specific convention you inferred
  - A workflow you had to figure out the hard way
  - A fact about the codebase (e.g. "migrations live in db/migrations/")

Use kind=rule for constraints, kind=workflow for procedures, kind=fact for project facts,
kind=note for context. Keep the body concise and actionable.

entries.name: go-verification-loop
entries.body:
When making any Go code change, follow this loop until all checks pass:

1. Write the code change.
2. Call the build tool. If it fails, fix compilation errors and go to step 2.
3. Call the vet tool. If it reports issues, fix them and go to step 2.
4. Call the test tool (or test_run for a targeted run). If tests fail, fix them and go to step 2.
5. If golangci-lint is enabled, call lint. Fix any reported issues and go to step 2.
6. All checks pass — the change is ready.

Use test_run {pattern} {pkg} to run a single test while iterating quickly, then
run the full test suite before finishing. Do not ask the user to run checks for you.

entries.name: hill-climbing
entries.body:
After completing a task, spend a moment asking: "Did I learn something this session that
should be encoded for next time?"

If yes, call add_entry with the lesson. The user will review it in the TUI (Review tab)
and approve, reject, or edit before it goes live. This is how the knowledge base improves
over time.

Do not propose trivial facts or things already in the knowledge base (call search_entries
first if unsure). Prefer quality over volume — one good entry beats five noisy ones.

entries.name: build
entries.body: Compile the Go project and return any build errors. Run this first in the verification sequence.

entries.name: vet
entries.body: Run go vet to catch common correctness issues (e.g. printf format mismatches, unreachable code). Run after build passes.

entries.name: test
entries.body: Run the full Go test suite. Use this as the final verification step. Returns pass/fail output for every package.

entries.name: test_run
entries.body:
Run a targeted subset of Go tests by name pattern and package. Use for fast iteration, then run the full test suite before finishing.

Examples:
  pattern=TestMyFunc  pkg=./internal/store
  pattern=TestAuth    pkg=./...

fields.params: [{"name":"pattern","description":"Test name regex (e.g. TestMyFunc or TestAuth)","required":true},{"name":"pkg","description":"Package path (e.g. ./internal/store or ./...)","required":true}]

entries.name: test_race
entries.body: Run the Go test suite with the race detector enabled. Use when investigating concurrency bugs or before shipping changes to concurrent code.

entries.name: lint
entries.body:
Run golangci-lint across the project. Catches style, complexity, and correctness issues beyond what vet reports.

Disabled by default. Enable by calling set_project with enable_lint=true once golangci-lint is installed.
